tests.output_sample:
Browse and call any API endpoint using the raw interface names.

The friendly top-level commands cover the most common operations.
This command provides access to ALL endpoints for power users and
agents that need full API coverage.

Run 'api' with no arguments to list all interfaces.
Run 'api <interface>' to see that interface's methods.

Usage:
  gisis-pp-cli api [interface] [flags]

Examples:
  # List all available interfaces
  gisis-pp-cli api

  # Show methods for a specific interface
  gisis-pp-cli api <interface-name>

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for api

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --max-age duration     Maximum acceptable age of local-store data before a stderr hint suggests sync; 0 disables (default 30m0s)
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'gisis-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable, default 2 for sniffed APIs) (default 2)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 1m0s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

tests.reason: command path [api] has fewer segments than placeholders (1)
command path [api] has fewer segments than placeholders (1)

Import data from a JSONL file by issuing POST requests for each record.
Each line must be a valid JSON object. Failed records are logged to stderr
but do not stop the import.

Usage:
  gisis-pp-cli import <resource> [flags]

Examples:
  # Import from a JSONL file
  gisis-pp-cli import <resource> --input data.jsonl

  # Dry-run to preview without sending
  gisis-pp-cli import <resource> --input data.jsonl --dry-run

  # Import from stdin
  cat data.jsonl | gisis-pp-cli import <resource> --input -

Flags:
      --batch-size int   Records per batch (future: batch API support) (default 1)
      --dry-run          Preview import without sending requests
  -h, --help             help for import
  -i, --input string     Input JSONL file path (use - for stdin)

Returns all locally cached vessels whose registered owner matches the given string. By default the match is exact (case-insensitive); pass --like for a substring match (useful when owner names vary in punctuation or suffixes).

Usage:
  gisis-pp-cli owner fleet <owner> [flags]

Examples:
  gisis-pp-cli owner fleet "KIVIK SHIPPING LTD" --json
  gisis-pp-cli owner fleet kivik --like

tests.output_sample:
List saved profiles

Usage:
  gisis-pp-cli profile list [flags]

Examples:
  gisis-pp-cli profile list
  gisis-pp-cli profile list --json

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for list

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --max-age duration     Maximum acceptable age of local-store data before a stderr hint suggests sync; 0 disables (default 30m0s)
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'gisis-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable, default 2 for sniffed APIs) (default 2)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 1m0s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)
NAME  FIELDS  DESCRIPTION

Captures every flag explicitly set on the invocation and stores
them under <name>. To update an existing profile, run save again; the
entry is replaced.

To avoid creating empty profiles, at least one non-default flag must be
present (other than --profile and --config).

Usage:
  gisis-pp-cli profile save <name> [--<flag> <value> ...] [flags]

Examples:
  gisis-pp-cli profile save my-defaults --json --compact
  gisis-pp-cli profile save tonight-defaults --region US

Flags:
      --description string   Short description shown in 'profile list'
  -h, --help                 help for save

Global Flags:

      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --max-age duration     Maximum acceptable age of local-store data before a stderr hint suggests sync; 0 disables (default 30m0s)
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'gisis-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable, default 2 for sniffed APIs) (default 2)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 1m0s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

Fetches each IMO's Ship Particulars in turn (default 2s between requests for GISIS politeness) and caches the result. Per-vessel failures are reported in the output without stopping the run, so a bad IMO never sinks the batch.

Usage:
  gisis-pp-cli ship batch [flags]

Examples:
  gisis-pp-cli ship batch --imos 9866641,9966233 --json
  gisis-pp-cli ship batch --file imos.txt --throttle 3s

Flags:
      --file string         Path to a file of IMO numbers (one per line or comma-separated)
  -h, --help                help for batch
      --imos string         Comma- or space-separated IMO numbers
      --throttle duration   Delay between requests for GISIS politeness (default 2s)

Global Flags:

      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --max-age duration     Maximum acceptable age of local-store data before a stderr hint suggests sync; 0 disables (default 30m0s)
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'gisis-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable, default 2 for sniffed APIs) (default 2)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 1m0s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

Fetches the GISIS Ship Particulars page for an IMO number and returns only the change history GISIS records inline — flag hops, renamings, type reclassifications, and ownership transfers — all from a single request. Also refreshes the local cache for that vessel.

  gisis-pp-cli ship history <imo> [flags]

Queries the local cache populated by 'ship get', 'ship batch', and 'ship refresh'. Combine filters to narrow the set; --name-like does a case-insensitive substring match on name or registered owner. Returns nothing if you have not fetched any vessels yet.

Usage:
  gisis-pp-cli ship list [flags]

Examples:
  gisis-pp-cli ship list --flag Panama --json
  gisis-pp-cli ship list --name-like sider --pinned

Flags:
      --flag string        Filter by flag state (exact, case-insensitive)
  -h, --help               help for list
      --limit int          Maximum number of rows to return (default 200)
      --name-like string   Substring search on vessel name or registered owner

Adds an IMO to the local watchlist so 'ship refresh --pinned' can re-fetch it on demand. Re-pinning updates the label. Use 'ship pin --list' to see the watchlist.

  gisis-pp-cli ship pin <imo> [flags]

Examples:
  gisis-pp-cli ship pin 9866641 --label "Lagos deal"
  gisis-pp-cli ship pin --list --json

      --list           List the watchlist instead of pinning

Re-fetches the given IMOs (positional args), all pinned vessels (--pinned), and/or cached vessels older than a threshold (--older-than), then updates the local cache. Forces a fresh fetch (bypasses the HTTP response cache) and honors the request throttle.

Usage:
  gisis-pp-cli ship refresh [imo...] [flags]

Examples:
  gisis-pp-cli ship refresh 9866641
  gisis-pp-cli ship refresh --pinned
  gisis-pp-cli ship refresh --older-than 30d

Flags:
  -h, --help                help for refresh
      --older-than string   Also refresh cached vessels older than this (e.g. 30d)
      --pinned              Refresh all pinned (watchlisted) vessels
      --throttle duration   Delay between requests for GISIS politeness (default 2s)

Scans the local cache and returns vessels whose last fetch is older than --older-than, oldest first. Pair with 'ship refresh --older-than' to bring them current. Use --pinned to limit the check to your watchlist.

  gisis-pp-cli ship stale --pinned --older-than 7d

GISIS is an HTML page-mode source with no list endpoint, so the generic
spec-driven sync template does not fit. Use the page-mode caching commands
instead: 'ship batch --imos ...' (fetch + cache many vessels), 'ship list'
(browse cache), 'ship pin <imo>' (watchlist), and 'ship refresh --pinned'
(re-fetch all pinned).

Usage:
  gisis-pp-cli sync [flags]

Examples:
  # sync is intentionally not implemented for GISIS — use ship batch instead
  gisis-pp-cli ship batch --imos 9866641,9123456 --json
  gisis-pp-cli ship list --json
  gisis-pp-cli ship refresh --pinned

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for sync

which resolves a natural-language capability query (for example, "search messages" or "stale tickets") to the best matching command from this CLI's curated feature index.

Exit codes:
  0  at least one match found
  2  no confident match - the query did not score against any indexed capability; fall back to '--help' or 'search' if this CLI has one

Usage:
  gisis-pp-cli which [query] [flags]

Examples:
  gisis-pp-cli which "stale tickets"
  gisis-pp-cli which "bottleneck"
  gisis-pp-cli which --limit 1 "send message"
  gisis-pp-cli which                                # list the full capability index

Flags:
  -h, --help        help for which
      --limit int   Maximum number of matches to return (default 3)
